description: Canonical parameter dictionary for junction field-effect transistors (JFETs), covering four sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) switching JFETs used as analog switches / choppers, which populate on_resistance and the drain_gate/source_gate on/off capacitances; (2) general amplifier JFETs (2N5457/58/59, J111/112/113, MMBFJ201, BF245); (3) low_noise JFETs (2N5457, LSK170, BF862, 2SK170) which additionally populate input_voltage_noise_density and noise_figure; and (4) dual_matched pairs (2N5564-66, LSK389, LSK489, IF3602) which populate idss_match_ratio, gate_source_voltage_match, and gate_isolation_current. A JFET is a DEPLETION-mode device and MUST NOT be modeled as an enhancement-mode MOSFET: its two defining specs are the zero-gate-voltage drain (saturation) current drain_saturation_current (IDSS) and the gate-source cutoff / pinch-off voltage gate_source_cutoff_voltage (VGS(off)/VP), which together set the transconductance curve; there is no enhancement threshold and no oxide, so the gate is a real reverse-biased p-n junction (hence the gate_reverse_current leakage and the forward_gate_current rating). IDSS carries a huge grade spread and is THE grade selector - never collapse its V_DS sweep, and emit one document per orderable grade. component.polarity carries the channel type: an N-channel JFET is 'positive', a P-channel JFET is 'negative' - store magnitudes, never let a P-channel datasheet's leading minus signs on VGS(off)/IDSS/VGS leak into the numbers; note the sign convention in conditionsVerbatim. A breakdown voltage that appears both as an absolute-maximum rating (VGS) and as a characterized value at a test current (V(BR)GSS at IG) is ONE key distinguished by limitClass, not two containers. Condition axes for this family: V_DS, V_GS, V_DG (drain-gate voltage, used for gate operating leakage), I_D (drain current, the sweep axis for VGS(off), gfs, and VGS), F (frequency, for noise and capacitance spot values), V_G1_G2 (gate-to-gate differential, dual-matched isolation), and T_A. Pin functions for a single JFET are G / D / S (reuse the FET convention; source and drain are often electrically interchangeable in a symmetric JFET - keep the datasheet's labeling); a dual-matched pair uses G1 / D1 / S1 / G2 / D2 / S2. Base-SI throughout: transconductance and output conductance use S (siemens = A/V; a printed umho normalizes to S, 1000 umho = 0.001 S), voltage-noise density uses V/sqrtHz (reuse the op_amp key name for cross-family normalization), and dimensionless match ratios use V/V. Full noise/transfer curves and the transfer-characteristic graph are graph-only and out of scope; capture the tabulated scalar figures. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Drain-gate voltage
parameters.hint: Maximum drain-gate voltage (absolute_max) - with the gate as the reverse-biased reference, this is the primary blocking rating of a JFET. On a P-channel part store the magnitude.

parameters.name: Gate-source voltage
parameters.hint: Maximum reverse gate-source voltage (absolute_max) - the reverse breakdown of the gate junction. Same key carries the absolute_max rating (VGS/VGSS) and, per limitClass, a characterized breakdown; VGSR denotes the reverse rating. Store the magnitude on a P-channel part.

parameters.name: Gate-drain voltage
parameters.hint: Maximum gate-drain voltage (absolute_max) where the datasheet tabulates it separately from the drain-gate rating (same junction, opposite reference). Store the magnitude on a P-channel part.

parameters.name: Forward gate current
parameters.hint: Maximum forward gate current (absolute_max) - the current allowed into the gate p-n junction when it is forward-biased. A JFET's gate is a real diode, so this is a genuine rating, unlike a MOSFET.

parameters.name: Total power dissipation
parameters.hint: Maximum total device dissipation, rated at a reference temperature (typically ambient T_A=25 C for a small-signal JFET). Emit one measurement per reference temperature (array over T_A) - a consumer must not apply the 25 C figure at elevated ambient without derating.

parameters.name: Gate-source breakdown voltage
parameters.hint: Characterized gate-source breakdown at a stated small gate current with the drain shorted to the source (V(BR)GSS at IG). The reverse breakdown of the gate junction; distinct from the gate_source_voltage absolute_max rating. Store the magnitude on a P-channel part.

parameters.name: Gate reverse (leakage) current
parameters.hint: Reverse gate leakage current at a stated reverse V_GS with the drain shorted to the source (array over V_GS and T_A - it roughly doubles every ~10 C). On low-noise / electrometer JFETs this is a headline sub-picoamp spec.

parameters.name: Gate operating current
parameters.hint: Gate leakage in the normal operating bias (drain-gate reverse-biased, drain current flowing), at a stated V_DG and I_D (array over V_DG). Lower than IGSS on some parts because self-heating differs; it is the dc bias-current a JFET input stage draws.

parameters.name: Gate-source cutoff (pinch-off) voltage
parameters.hint: THE defining spec (with IDSS): the gate-source voltage that pinches the channel off to a stated small drain current (VGS(off) at I_D, at a fixed V_DS). VP/pinch-off is the same quantity read as a magnitude. Array over the I_D test point. Wide grade spread; store the magnitude on a P-channel part.

parameters.name: Gate-source voltage (operating)
parameters.hint: Gate-source voltage required to set a stated operating drain current at a fixed V_DS (array over I_D) - the point on the transfer curve between VGS(off) and IDSS. Distinct from the gate_source_voltage absolute_max rating.

parameters.name: Drain cutoff current
parameters.hint: Drain leakage with the channel pinched off (V_GS beyond cutoff), at a stated V_DS - the OFF-state leakage of a JFET analog switch.

parameters.name: Zero-gate-voltage drain (saturation) current
parameters.hint: THE defining spec: the drain current with VGS=0 (gate shorted to source) at a stated V_DS in saturation (array over V_DS). Carries a huge grade spread and is the primary grade selector - emit one document per orderable grade and never collapse the V_DS sweep.

parameters.name: Drain-source on resistance
parameters.hint: Small-signal drain-source resistance in the ohmic region at VGS=0 (or a stated V_GS) - the ON resistance of a JFET analog switch. No SI prefix; a printed value in ohms.

parameters.name: Forward transconductance
parameters.hint: Forward transconductance (dID/dVGS) at a stated I_D (or VGS=0 for gfs0) and V_DS, at a test frequency (array over I_D). Unit S = siemens (A/V); a printed umho / micromho normalizes to S (1000 umho = 0.001 S). yfs is the same as the small-signal transfer admittance magnitude at low frequency.

parameters.name: Output conductance
parameters.hint: Common-source output conductance (dID/dVDS) at a stated bias and frequency (siemens). Sets the intrinsic output impedance of a JFET gain stage.

parameters.name: Input capacitance
parameters.hint: Common-source short-circuit input capacitance at a stated V_DS, VGS=0, and test frequency (array over V_DS). Includes the gate-source and Miller gate-drain terms.

parameters.name: Reverse transfer (feedback) capacitance
parameters.hint: Common-source short-circuit reverse-transfer (gate-drain, Miller-feedback) capacitance at a stated bias and frequency. Limits the stable high-frequency gain.

parameters.name: Output capacitance
parameters.hint: Common-source output (drain-source plus gate-drain) capacitance at a stated V_DS and test frequency (array over V_DS).

parameters.name: Drain-gate on-state capacitance
parameters.hint: Switching JFET: drain-gate capacitance in the ON (conducting) state at a stated bias and frequency - sets charge injection when the switch turns off.

parameters.name: Drain-gate off-state capacitance
parameters.hint: Switching JFET: drain-gate capacitance in the OFF (pinched-off) state at a stated reverse V_GS and frequency - sets OFF-state feedthrough.

parameters.name: Source-gate off-state capacitance
parameters.hint: Switching JFET: source-gate capacitance in the OFF (pinched-off) state at a stated reverse V_GS and frequency.

parameters.name: Input voltage-noise density
parameters.hint: Equivalent input voltage-noise density at a stated I_D / V_DS and frequency (array over F - JFETs have a 1/f corner, so tabulate the spot values). Unit V/sqrtHz; reuse this op_amp key name for cross-family normalization. The headline spec of a low_noise JFET.

parameters.name: Noise figure
parameters.hint: Noise figure at a stated bias, source resistance, and frequency (array over F). NFmin is the minimum over source matching.

parameters.name: IDSS match ratio (dual)
parameters.hint: Dual-matched pair: the dimensionless ratio of the two channels' IDSS (V/V), a measure of how closely the two JFETs are matched. Often specified as a minimum (e.g. 0.95). Only meaningful on a dual part.

parameters.name: Gate-source voltage match (dual)
parameters.hint: Dual-matched pair: the differential |VGS1 - VGS2| at a stated common drain current - the DC offset of a JFET-input differential stage. Store the magnitude.

parameters.name: Gate-to-gate isolation current (dual)
parameters.hint: Dual-matched pair: the leakage current between the two gates at a stated gate-to-gate differential voltage (array over V_G1_G2) - the isolation between the two matched channels.

parameters.name: Junction-to-case thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-case thermal resistance (power / metal-can JFETs).

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance (K/W and degC/W are identical; normalize to degC/W).

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: The operating (junction or ambient) temperature range (min/max).

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range (min/max).
